query: My eww widget is anchored 'center left' and I set :y to 150px but it won't move down vertically no matter what value I use. The :x offset works fine. What's wrong?
expected_behavior: Routes to POSITIONING.md (Anchor Edge vs Anchor Center section)
Explains that single-edge anchors ('center left'/'center right') LOCK the perpendicular axis — the compositor centers along the unnamed axis and silently ignores the :y offset
Does NOT suggest tweaking the :y value harder or blame eww/GTK — identifies the layer-shell anchor semantics as the cause
Fixes it by switching to a corner anchor ('top left' / 'bottom left') so both axes have a fixed reference, converting the desired y into a corner-relative coordinate
notes: A naive answer keeps adjusting the :y value (the symptom) instead of recognizing that 'center left' is a layer-shell constraint that locks the vertical axis. The skill knows the perpendicular axis is locked and the only fix is a corner anchor — without it the user burns time fighting an offset the compositor discards.
